reasoning: The flagged 'auth.rs' infinite loops and sudo bypass reported by deepseek are false positives - they're runtime behaviors of the compiled binary, not executed during makepkg build/install. Both models confirmed PKGBUILD and build scripts are safe.
disagreement_analysis: Deepseek incorrectly flagged auth.rs runtime behavior as a build-time threat, while Qwen didn't review auth.rs but covered other critical files without issues.
coverage_issues: Qwen failed to review auth.rs (critical privilege-handling code)
Deepseek skipped paru-2.1.0.tar.gz source archive (should be inspected via src extraction)
false_positives: Deepseek's auth.rs verdict: Infinite loops affect runtime operation only, not build process
Deepseek's auth.rs sudo bypass: Normal privilege escalation pattern for AUR helpers
false_negatives: Both models missed reviewing .install hooks (none exist but should be verified)
Qwen didn't examine auth.rs privilege handling logic
learnings: Prioritize build-executed files: PKGBUILD > .install hooks > build-script-referenced source
Improve false-positive filtering: Distinguish build-time vs runtime behaviors in assessment
Require archive inspection: Extract source tarballs instead of skipping binary files
Enforce critical file review: Privilege/auth handling code must always be assessed
